identity.short_name: Sum of squares of achievable cycle lengths in a side-4 triangular grid

problem_spec.summary: In a side-4 triangular grid of unit triangles, robot closed paths with no immediate backtrack and "length = number of distinct visited cells" correspond to simple cycles in the dual adjacency graph. Use bipartite parity and a dual-face enclosure/perimeter model (bounded faces correspond to interior grid vertices) to enumerate achievable cycle lengths, then compute the sum of their squares.
givens: T is an equilateral triangle of side length 4 partitioned into unit equilateral triangle cells.
Two cells are adjacent iff they share an edge; G is the resulting adjacency (dual) graph.
A robot moves between adjacent cells and never moves to the cell it occupied immediately before.
For integer k, we ask whether there exists a closed path of length k visiting exactly k distinct cells.
ask: Let S be the set of achievable k. Compute out = Σ_{k\inS} k^2 and output it as a three-digit zero-padded integer.

parameter(s).name: side_length
parameter(s).purpose: fixes a small finite dual-face configuration enabling complete by-hand enumeration

parameter(s).name: move_rule
parameter(s).purpose: rules out immediate reversals; with distinct-vertex requirement this encodes simple-cycle behavior

parameter(s).name: aggregate_function
parameter(s).purpose: changes the postprocessing from Σk to Σk^2, altering the final output while preserving the same structural enumeration

invariant(s).name: dual_graph_bipartite
invariant(s).statement: The dual adjacency graph is bipartite by triangle orientation (up vs down), so every cycle length is even.

invariant(s).name: bounded_faces_correspond_to_interior_vertices
invariant(s).statement: In a planar triangulation, bounded faces of the dual correspond to interior vertices of the primal. For side length 4, there are exactly 3 interior vertices, hence exactly 3 bounded dual faces, each a 6-cycle.

invariant(s).name: perimeter_of_union_of_bounded_faces
invariant(s).statement: Any simple cycle is the boundary of the union of bounded faces it encloses. If m faces are enclosed and e dual edges are shared between enclosed faces, then the boundary length is k = 6m - 2e.
